rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use assign statements in implementation scope only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes corresponding to their semantic level.
Guarantee statements should relate visible inputs to visible outputs in component type contracts.
Implementation-level contracts should reference subcomponents and connections only in implementation scope.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantees in type scope should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables.
Do not treat AADL event ports as numeric or Boolean values in AGREE expressions.
